level_signals: 5+ years of experience in incident management, technical program management, or a related coordination-heavy role
First dedicated incident response program hire with direct partnership with executive leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in incident management, technical program management, or a related coordination-heavy role within a software or security organization.
Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams through high-pressure situations with composure, clarity, and strong interpersonal judgment.
Sufficient technical depth to understand distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, and security concepts at a level that enables effective triage and stakeholder translation.
Exceptional organizational skills — able to manage multiple concurrent workstreams, track dependencies, and drive accountability without direct authority.
Experience building incident response or operational programs from scratch, including defining processes, documentation, and success metrics in ambiguous environments.
High emotional intelligence and the communication skills to operate credibly with engineers, executives, legal counsel, and external stakeholders alike.

scope.surface: Detection & Response function within Harvey's Information Security organization; security and reliability incident management across the AI-native SaaS platform
scope.business_model: enterprise SaaS, B2B, AI-native platform for legal and professional services
primary_metrics_inferred: Incident readiness (tabletop exercise outcomes, on-call improvements)
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end coordination of security and reliability incidents — from initial triage through resolution — ensuring clear roles, rapid decision-making, and structured communication across engineering, security, legal, and customer-facing teams.
Design and implement incident management processes, runbooks, and escalation frameworks that scale with Harvey's growth and reflect the realities of operating AI-native SaaS infrastructure.
Lead post-incident reviews and drive remediation programs to completion, tracking follow-up items across teams and holding stakeholders accountable to timelines.
Build and maintain incident readiness — including tabletop exercises, on-call coordination improvements, and tooling for real-time situational awareness.
Serve as the communication hub during active incidents, translating technical status into clear updates for executives, legal, and customer success without losing fidelity.

green_flags: First dedicated hire in the role — high visibility and ownership opportunity
Direct partnership with security engineering, product, and executive leadership
Clear mission and responsibilities described for the role
Harvey has strong product-market fit with 1500+ customers in 60+ countries
red_flags: Location type not specified — remote eligibility, hybrid expectations, and cities are all unclear
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
No equity or benefits details mentioned beyond base comp range
Equity not mentioned despite being a growth-stage AI company where equity is typically a major component
